surfaces.removalGate: A replacement operating frame covers setup, validation, workflow, and security boundaries.
surfaces.rationale: The root frame owns repository-wide conventions; Copilot instructions own editor-specific routing.

surfaces.removalGate: A generated scope view reproduces every applyTo binding without becoming a second source.
surfaces.rationale: Instruction files remain authoritative, but their overlapping scopes need one computed audit view.
surfaces.followUp: Generate the instruction scope view during the guidance modernization slice.

surfaces.removalGate: Equivalent versioned user guidance exists and all inbound links are migrated.
surfaces.rationale: User documentation is distinct from repository-authoring and runtime prompt guidance.

surfaces.removalGate: A replacement preserves managed roles, models, interaction types, and invocation edges.
surfaces.rationale: The managed manifest owns roles and invocation edges while agent frontmatter remains executable definition.

surfaces.removalGate: A replacement preserves the model allowlist, assignments, deprecations, and consistency proof.
surfaces.rationale: Definitions are maintained centrally and assignments are generated from executable frontmatter.

surfaces.removalGate: A generated skill view reproduces discovery metadata without hand-maintained duplication.
surfaces.rationale: Skill frontmatter stays canonical; a computed consumer view should replace repeated inventory prose.
surfaces.followUp: Generate the skill consumer view during invocation-graph modernization.

surfaces.removalGate: A replacement preserves all nodes, edges, gates, artifacts, and track routing.
surfaces.rationale: The DAG is the machine-readable owner of workflow order and handoffs.

surfaces.removalGate: Generated VS Code and Copilot CLI projections preserve roles, interaction boundaries, MCP allowlists, and equivalent typed outcomes.
surfaces.rationale: One managed role graph must produce client-specific mechanics without creating separate workflow authorities.
surfaces.followUp: Issue #111 generates and locks both workspace projections; live normalized outcome parity remains a qualification gate.

surfaces.removalGate: Versioned replacements support deterministic migration and package validation.
surfaces.rationale: Versioned manifests separate runtime configuration from repository authoring details.

surfaces.removalGate: A replacement package graph preserves public exports, build order, and clean installation.
surfaces.rationale: Workspace packages are the executable ownership units for contracts, kernel, CLI, and adapters.

surfaces.removalGate: Every persisted schema has one declared source and generated-byte parity before either library changes.
surfaces.rationale: TypeBox contracts and repository JSON schemas overlap in purpose but serve different consumers.
surfaces.followUp: Map generated versus hand-authored schemas before choosing consolidation boundaries.

surfaces.removalGate: Both client projections install, update, roll back, and uninstall atomically from one npm-owned manifest and content lock.
surfaces.rationale: The approved lifecycle extends the existing npm and manifest authority rather than adding APM or another installer.
surfaces.followUp: Issue #111 adds deterministic client projections and shared lifecycle coverage; live two-client outcome qualification remains pending.

surfaces.removalGate: Each retired server has a passing replacement gate, zero active discovery or package references, and an explicit negative assertion.
surfaces.rationale: MCP ownership is currently distributed across client config, packs, setup, tests, and documentation.
surfaces.followUp: Create one typed descriptor and retire Astro, Terraform, Azure Pricing, and Draw.io integrations independently.

surfaces.removalGate: Typed normalized ARM MCP evidence meets pricing parity, reliability, latency, determinism, security, and maintenance gates before the custom pack is archived.
surfaces.rationale: Managed pricing trust should belong to versioned APEX evidence contracts, not raw upstream MCP output.
surfaces.followUp: Add pricing request and evidence contracts, a read-allowlisted ARM MCP adapter, and restricted raw-evidence attestation.

surfaces.removalGate: Allowlisted outcome ingestion is opt-in, deterministic, redacted, idempotent, measured, and unable to mutate repository or runtime policy.
surfaces.rationale: Existing inert improvement contracts need a bounded outcome adapter and defensible measurements, not a second learning store.
surfaces.followUp: Map journal and evidence outcomes to stable observations and measure precision, duplication, recurrence, quarantine, storage, and triage cost.

surfaces.removalGate: A replacement maps every validator ID to one executable owner and boundary.
surfaces.rationale: This is the runtime authorization map, not merely a repository lint registry.

surfaces.removalGate: Graph-backed projections preserve command names, prerequisites, parallel safety, aliases, consumers, and check diagnostics before duplicate orchestration is removed.
surfaces.rationale: The graph owns validation metadata while package scripts remain the executable projection and hooks/workflows retain environment-specific behavior.
surfaces.followUp: Issue #105 delegates graph-governed public aliases to canonical npm scripts; aggregate and thin-hook projections remain independent later slices.

surfaces.removalGate: Replacement checks retain language-native parsing and equivalent failure diagnostics.
surfaces.rationale: Language tools should not be reimplemented by generic string validators.

surfaces.removalGate: Hooks and hosted checks call repository-owned commands and preserve lint scope, diagnostics, exclusions, and nonzero failures when tooling is unavailable.
surfaces.rationale: Audience-specific authoring guidance is distinct from executable lint policy, but hooks and workflows should consume the same npm-owned command contract.
surfaces.followUp: Issue #95 repairs the fail-open Markdown hook lookup and adds missing-executable regression coverage before any lint-scope change.

surfaces.removalGate: One template-derived heading model replaces hand-synchronized heading lists with parity tests.
surfaces.rationale: Templates are canonical; runtime and compact heading registries are deterministic generated derivatives with explicit non-template exceptions.
surfaces.followUp: Issue #115 derives both heading registries from templates, replaces JavaScript source regex parsing with structured metadata, and adds byte-parity and mutation tests.

surfaces.removalGate: Missing executables fail nonzero, staged-file scope and diagnostics match direct npm commands, and index-lock coordination permits safe parallel execution with unchanged staged-file semantics.
surfaces.rationale: Serial execution protects the model-catalog and SKU-manifest generators, which both re-stage output through one Git index; read-only hooks must not claim stage_fixed ownership.
surfaces.followUp: Issues #95 and #113 make Markdown fail closed, remove its unnecessary stage_fixed ownership, delegate Terraform formatting to its canonical npm command, and prove a held index lock rejects a concurrent writer. Serial execution remains required unless explicit coordination is added.

surfaces.removalGate: Replacement checks retain stable names, least privilege, exact triggers, and equivalent commands.
surfaces.rationale: GitHub branch protection owns required contexts, workflows own hosted execution, and npm scripts own reusable command implementation.

surfaces.removalGate: A replacement checks out the exact candidate without persisted credentials and preserves compact evidence, checksums, retention, denial of release authority, and separate hosted visibility.
surfaces.rationale: Release qualification has a longer evidence-producing trust boundary than required deterministic CI and must remain independently visible.

surfaces.removalGate: Equivalent scheduled ownership exists for every freshness and maintenance task.
surfaces.rationale: Scheduled automation is distinct from required pull-request checks.

surfaces.removalGate: No active package command, source path, or repository consumer remains; workflow files stay byte-identical and archive provenance records source hash and rollback.
surfaces.rationale: Upstream workflow copying belongs to the former accelerator model and conflicts with standalone repository workflow ownership.
surfaces.followUp: Retain archive bytes and provenance; restore only through the documented rollback after a new ownership decision.

surfaces.removalGate: Replacement generation is byte-deterministic and preserves hand-maintained model definitions.
surfaces.rationale: Assignments are derived data and should remain generated.

surfaces.removalGate: A replacement computes every tracked entity count from canonical globs.
surfaces.rationale: Exact entity counts are derived facts and must not be copied into prose.

surfaces.removalGate: Replacement preserves Bicep and Terraform pin freshness with offline validation.
surfaces.rationale: The index is generated cache data; module declarations remain canonical in IaC.

surfaces.removalGate: Replacement tracks source canaries, review dates, and stale references with equivalent diagnostics.
surfaces.rationale: Freshness metadata is a dedicated drift-control surface.

surfaces.removalGate: Format-neutral scenarios prove editable Mermaid or Python source, semantic and visual quality, deterministic rendering, and historical Draw.io readability.
surfaces.rationale: New inline and standalone diagrams need explicit format owners before the Draw.io toolchain can retire.
surfaces.followUp: Generalize golden scenarios, route inline output to Mermaid, route standalone output to Python, then archive Draw.io after measured parity.

surfaces.removalGate: One deterministic generation path records source mappings, client projections, hashes, versions, composition provenance, and package inventory.
surfaces.rationale: Generated install payloads must stay derived from canonical customizations inside the npm lifecycle.
surfaces.followUp: Issues #99 and #111 add source mappings, composition provenance, aggregate and per-client locks, and deterministic projections without APM or a second authority.

surfaces.removalGate: Issue #13 accepts cutover and every preserved or changed behavior has replacement proof.
surfaces.rationale: The matrix is required through modernization but becomes historical after accepted cutover.
surfaces.followUp: Archive, do not delete, after the final cutover decision.

surfaces.removalGate: Every alias and legacy command has usage evidence, replacement guidance, and a dated removal decision.
surfaces.rationale: Public command compatibility cannot be inferred safely from implementation names alone.
surfaces.followUp: Inventory aliases and command consumers during the compatibility slice.

surfaces.removalGate: All validator families emit stable severity, path, summary, and machine-readable diagnostics.
surfaces.rationale: Shared reporter adoption is partial, producing inconsistent output and remediation context.
surfaces.followUp: Issue #117 migrates the registry and hosted-workflow contract family with text and JSON output snapshots; remaining families migrate independently.

surfaces.removalGate: Automatic collection yields representative elapsed, token, retry, subagent, and cache samples.
surfaces.rationale: The aggregator exists, but the current repository contains no telemetry records.
surfaces.followUp: Instrument apex-recall checkpoints before claiming workflow savings.

surfaces.removalGate: A privacy-safe supported source supplies repeatable context measurements or the profiler is explicitly retired.
surfaces.rationale: The profiler depends on local debug logs and is not a portable CI baseline.
surfaces.followUp: Decide whether local profiling remains advisory or becomes supported telemetry.

surfaces.removalGate: Both client projections receive equivalent kernel, authority, MCP trust, and workflow guidance without duplicating repository-authoring rules.
surfaces.rationale: Shipped consumer behavior needs one managed guidance owner distinct from repository contributor instructions.
surfaces.followUp: Rewrite managed guidance after client and MCP behavior is implemented, then regenerate packaged assets.

baselines.evidence: At b27d173, active repository and managed guidance Markdown/JSON totals 3793215 bytes; static budget rules pass, but the repository has no representative runtime token or cache records.
baselines.followUp: Milestone O must collect both-client token and cache samples before claiming context improvement.

baselines.measurement: Current-main runs queried with gh run list; PR checks and branch protection queried with gh pr checks and gh api.
baselines.evidence: Run 29898484667 passed at b27d173 in 122 seconds. Main protection is strict and requires ci, both external Python test and three CodeQL contexts; PR #92 also preserved separate docs, branch-enforcement, CodeQL summary, and exact-head qualification checks.

baselines.measurement: npm run validate:hooks; npm run test:hooks
baselines.evidence: At b27d173, direct missing Markdown tooling exits 127 while the current hook wrapper pattern prints the same lookup error and exits 0; serial stage_fixed execution also lacks a representative timing benchmark.
baselines.followUp: Issue #95 owns the fail-closed regression and repair; representative documentation, code, workflow, and artifact timing benchmarks remain a later Milestone N slice.

baselines.evidence: Lock SHA-256 5727e5fd6353b31b347cffaf6c537a5c0a6be20ce2460d66125d09b118f8b525; workspace package boundaries recorded by package manifests.

baselines.measurement: Reporter import scan; npm run test:lib-json; npm run test:lib-ajv; npm run test:registry-validator-reporter
baselines.evidence: Shared diagnostic library tests pass. Issue #117 snapshots stable text and JSON diagnostics for the modernization ownership, repository validator graph, and hosted workflow contract family; repository-wide reporter adoption remains partial.
baselines.followUp: Migrate each remaining validator family only after capturing its output and exit contract.

baselines.measurement: Thirty-day git path concentration plus quarterly drift validators.
baselines.evidence: Recent churn is concentrated in skills, tools, packages, docs, instructions, agents, and workflows; generated freshness, count, and model checks are the existing drift gates.

decisions.decision: Keep skill and instruction frontmatter canonical; generate consumer and scope views.
decisions.rationale: A hand-maintained second registry would create new drift instead of consolidating ownership.
decisions.gate: Generated views match discovery metadata and existing validators remain green.

decisions.decision: Generate validator orchestration views from explicit command dependencies.
decisions.rationale: Package scripts are reusable implementation, but repeated orchestration lists obscure prerequisites and parallel safety.
decisions.gate: Characterization tests preserve commands, outputs, ordering constraints, and required check names.

decisions.decision: Retain serial hooks until an explicit Git index coordination design is proven.
decisions.rationale: Parallel stage_fixed writers race on the Git index; speed cannot override staged-file correctness.
decisions.gate: Representative benchmarks and race tests pass with coordinated parallel execution.

decisions.decision: Derive artifact heading metadata from canonical templates.
decisions.rationale: Hand-synchronized heading arrays duplicate template ownership and create drift.
decisions.gate: Generated metadata is byte-stable and all artifact characterization tests pass.

decisions.decision: Do not merge TypeBox and repository JSON schema libraries until source and consumer provenance is mapped.
decisions.rationale: The libraries overlap conceptually but serve runtime packages and repository artifacts differently.
decisions.gate: Every schema has one source, generation path, consumer set, compatibility rule, and byte-parity test.

decisions.decision: Keep npm and customizations/manifest.json authoritative while extending transactional distribution to both supported clients.
decisions.rationale: Source, generated, lock, composition, and rollback concepts fit the existing lifecycle without adding APM or a second installer.
decisions.gate: Source-to-generated-to-install provenance, two-client lifecycle, conflict, rollback, package, SBOM, and reproducibility tests pass.

decisions.decision: Retire the v1 compatibility matrix from active gates only after accepted cutover, then archive it.
decisions.rationale: The matrix protects modernization behavior until the maintainer accepts the final candidate.
decisions.gate: Issue #13 closes with replacement evidence for every preserved and changed behavior.

decisions.decision: Consolidate validator diagnostics on the shared reporter by validator family.
decisions.rationale: Stable structured diagnostics improve local repair and CI interpretation without combining validation semantics.
decisions.gate: Output snapshots and machine-readable findings remain compatible for each migrated family.

decisions.decision: Treat workflow telemetry and debug-log profiling as advisory until representative automatic samples exist.
decisions.rationale: No optimization claim is defensible from an empty telemetry set or machine-local debug logs.
decisions.gate: Privacy-safe automated samples cover representative tiers, tracks, retries, cache use, and context.

decisions.decision: Retain public CLI compatibility until aliases and external consumers are enumerated.
decisions.rationale: Removing an apparently unused alias can break consumer automation outside this repository.
decisions.gate: Usage evidence, replacement guidance, and a dated removal decision exist for every alias.

decisions.decision: Generate VS Code and Copilot CLI projections from one managed customization manifest.
decisions.rationale: Client-specific interaction mechanics must not fork typed workflow, state, gate, evidence, or authority ownership.
decisions.gate: Both supported clients pass the same normalized workflow and authorization corpus on pinned versions.

decisions.decision: Manage the MCP portfolio through typed APEX descriptors and per-server replacement gates.
decisions.rationale: Configuration, setup, packs, tests, and guidance must retire together without granting raw upstream tools managed trust.
decisions.gate: Each retired MCP is absent from active discovery, setup, packaging, tests, and guidance after replacement proof passes.

decisions.decision: Make versioned normalized evidence contracts authoritative for managed Azure pricing.
decisions.rationale: Typed attestation can preserve pricing semantics and provenance while restricting raw ARM MCP output and denying writes.
decisions.gate: The ARM adapter passes parity, reliability, latency, security, determinism, arithmetic, and maintenance criteria.

decisions.decision: Route new inline diagrams to Mermaid and standalone diagrams to editable Python sources.
decisions.rationale: The two formats cover documentation and inspectable architecture outputs without retaining Draw.io generation.
decisions.gate: Format-neutral semantic and visual qualification passes before Draw.io generation is archived.

decisions.decision: Ingest only allowlisted structured APEX outcomes into the existing inert improvement lifecycle.
decisions.rationale: Measured recurrence can improve triage without transcript scraping, context injection, or mutation authority.
decisions.gate: Privacy, precision, deduplication, recurrence, retention, provenance, and no-mutation thresholds pass.

decisions.decision: Keep CLI asset preparation as the only generated bundle path inside npm distribution.
decisions.rationale: One deterministic generator can add source mappings, client locks, and composition provenance without another package manager.
decisions.gate: Generated client assets, package inventory, manifest, SBOM, provenance, and tarballs are deterministic and lifecycle-tested.

decisions.decision: Keep shipped consumer guidance in managed customizations and project it consistently to both clients.
decisions.rationale: Consumer workflow rules are distinct from repository-authoring instructions and should not be duplicated across clients.
decisions.gate: Both clients discover equivalent managed guidance with no obsolete MCP, Draw.io, VS Code-only, or authority claim.

decisions.decision: Keep Markdown authoring guidance audience-specific while making npm scripts the shared executable lint contract.
decisions.rationale: Human docs, agents, skills, templates, and frozen artifacts need different authoring rules, but hook and CI failure behavior must not fork.
decisions.gate: Direct, hook, editor, and hosted checks preserve scope and diagnostics and fail nonzero when the executable is missing.

decisions.decision: Retire upstream workflow synchronization and preserve its source only as provenance.
decisions.rationale: Standalone workflow files and their validated hosted contract replace network-dependent copying from the former upstream repository.
decisions.gate: Active references and command entrypoints are absent, workflow bytes remain unchanged, and archived source hash plus rollback are recorded.
